items.md: ![Prince Valiant comic strip panel by Harold R. Foster showing a figure standing on a rocky cliff overlooking a stormy sea.](CBatAotCB-062_page_1_image_2_v2.jpg)

items.caption: In his "morgue" of reference materials, which included the files shown in a 1969 snapshot, Barks stored the work of such comic-strip masters as Harold Foster and Alex Raymond. He clipped many panels of water scenes from Foster's "Prince Valiant," and their influence can be felt in his depiction of the rugged coast of Labrador in "The Golden Helmet" in <i>Donald Duck</> Four Color No. 408, 1952. In the same story, Barks made the museum where Donald worked as a guard seem just as real as the coast of Labrador—but by introducing fantastic rather than realistic details. "The Golden Helmet" © 1952 Walt Disney Productions; "Prince Valiant" © 1951 King Features Syndicate.
